overview:
EVOLVE the proven machine pool; do not rewrite it. Verified ground truth in the worktree (re-read this turn): BoxRecord (worker-box-pool/src/types.ts:161) fuses a long-lived MACHINE with a per-run LEASE stamped at pool.ts stamp() (601-632) and unwound at onLeaseSettle() (868-934); maxInFlight (domain/src/index.ts:192, parsed config:594 positiveInt default 1) already lets one machine carry N leases and box-second billing is already hardened (liveLeaseAcquiredMs windows). The ONLY per-run resource NOT isolated is the MCP endpoint, and CRUCIALLY it is NOT just a tunnel: acquireAgentMcpEndpoint (mcp/src/agentEndpoint.ts:34-68) bundles THREE sub-resources behind one lease - (1) an auth token (issueMcpToken/revokeMcpToken), (2) a refcounted LOCAL mcp server (ensureLocalMcpServer / localMcpServers Map / releaseLocalMcpServer, 114-149), and (3) the reverse tunnel (workerHostPool.acquireRemoteMcpTunnel, host-keyed singleton index.ts:21-71). All three are acquired in acp.startSession (acp/src/index.ts:81) and ALL THREE are released together in acp.stopSession via `await session.mcpEndpoint.release()` (acp:280), which runs inside agent-runner's `finally { await session.stop() }` (agent-runner:180), itself inside runtime's `this.runner(...)` (runtime:544). That is why maxInFlight=1 is today's only safe value.

The central correction over the prior plan: the per-run resource the coordinator must own is the WHOLE AgentMcpEndpointLease (token+local-server-ref+tunnel), NOT 'the tunnel'. Splitting only the tunnel orphans the token and the local-server refcount. So the coordinator owns an injected McpEndpointManager that calls the EXISTING acquireAgentMcpEndpoint once per run (open-after-bind) and that lease's release() once per run (close-before-settle); acp is patched to SKIP its own acquire AND its own release whenever a pre-resolved endpoint is threaded in (a single ownership flag, not a partial split). An explicit ownership table governs {local-host, remote single-slot, remote N-slot}.

THE PLAN (every step keeps the full suite green; default slotsPerMachine=1 makes the coordinator a 1:1 byte-identical passthrough at every step):
(STEP 0, pure rename) alias BoxRecord->MachineLease (BoxRecord stays canonical), rename maxInFlight->slotsPerMachine in domain with maxInFlight kept as a derived getter (NOT a plain dual field, to prevent drift); config key worker.box_pool.max_in_flight unchanged.
(STEP 1, 1:1 passthrough) NEW package @symphony/dispatch-coordinator (distinct from the existing pure-policy @symphony/dispatch) wrapping BoxPool; mint a RunSlot with mcpEndpoint=null; reproduce acquire->setWorkerHost->settle AND the EXACT two runtime event strings (box_pool_acquire_error on throw, worker_host_capacity on every no_capacity reason). The runtime keeps accepting input.boxPool (lower-churn): when a bare BoxPool is injected the runtime wraps it in a null-endpoint passthrough coordinator internally, so every existing test injection site stays untouched. The coordinator and its registry are a reload-surviving singleton built once in daemon/main.
(STEP 2, per-run endpoint) coordinator opens the WHOLE endpoint lease after lease-bind and closes it before lease-settle via the injected McpEndpointManager; acp consumes slot.mcpEndpoint and skips its own acquire+release. WorkerHostPool gains per-run-keyed openForRun/closeForRun (re-keyed by `${workerHost}#${runKey}`, distinct remotePort, no host-coalescing) used by the manager for the remote tunnel; host-keyed methods stay for the single-tenant/local fallback. A POOL-OWNED ordering invariant guarantees machine recycle cannot tear a box out from under a still-open run endpoint, and a poisoned machine returns a CLEAN failure to siblings.
(STEP 3, unlock >1) a TWO-PART gate: validateDispatchConfig keeps the static structural checks, and a NEW post-construction capability gate in main.ts (after the coordinator exists) rejects slotsPerMachine>1 unless the coordinator advertises perRunEndpoint AND the operator set an explicit co-residence opt-in flag (blast-radius is an explicit tradeoff, not just a capability). Force the gated unconditional slot suffix for same-issue co-residence (both local and remote workspace paths), with a verified (issueId, slotIndex) uniqueness invariant feeding the runKey. Add a derived max-concurrent-tunnels ceiling that surfaces as a typed no_capacity, not a throw.
(STEP 4, Finding #1) BoxPool.swapProvider on reconcile, with the old provider CAPTURED PER BOX (BoxRecord.originProvider) so an in-flight lease that settles after the swap routes destroy() to its ORIGINAL provider and never orphans a paid box.

Build/verify gates after every step: pnpm typecheck (tsc --build) && pnpm exec vitest run && pnpm lint (run pnpm directly, NOT mise, per repo memory).

packageLayout.purpose: STEP 0: add `export type MachineLease = BoxRecord;` next to BoxRecord (161); BoxRecord stays canonical so all provider drivers + the green pool/lease/ledger/mutex/reaper/registry suites compile unchanged; BoxLease (223-232) retained verbatim as the machine-side sub-handle the RunSlot delegates settle to. STEP 4: add `originProvider?: BoxProvider` to BoxRecord (the provider that PROVISIONED this box) so recycle()/destroy route to the box's ORIGINAL backend after a provider swap, never the new this.provider.

STEP 0: isLeasable/pickRecord/canAcquire (56,257,574) read settings.slotsPerMachine (the renamed field). STEP 4: add `swapProvider(next): void` that (a) captures originProvider on every existing record BEFORE reassigning (each surviving box already carries the provider it was provisioned by), (b) marks old-provider boxes for drain, (c) reassigns this.provider=resolveProvider(next.provider,next,deps) (drop `readonly` at 137), (d) re-threads reaperInternals.provider, (e) rebuilds the ledger usesLedger gate WITHOUT reconstructing the singleton; and modify recycle() (969-999) + destroyDescriptor to call `(record.originProvider ?? this.provider).destroy(...)` so an in-flight lease settling after the swap destroys against its ORIGINAL backend. The hardened internals (inventory/boxMutexes/reservations/reaper/runDrain/ledger/spend/onLeaseSettle) stay byte-for-byte. STEP 2 ordering hook: onLeaseSettle stays pool-owned; the coordinator registers an optional `onMachineRecycling(boxId)` callback the pool invokes INSIDE the per-box mutex immediately before recycle()'s provider.destroy, so the coordinator can fail-fast any still-open RunSlots/endpoints bound to that machine (the recycle-vs-endpoint ordering invariant).

STEP 2: add openForRun(workerHost, runKey, localHost, localPort) / closeForRun(workerHost, runKey) that re-key remoteMcpTunnels by `${workerHost}#${runKey}`, allocate a DISTINCT remotePort per run via the existing nextRemoteMcpPort/availableRemoteMcpPorts allocator, and DO NOT coalesce by host. The localPort-swap-on-reload hazard (26-36) is handled per-run: a per-run entry is matched on its full `${workerHost}#${runKey}` key and is NEVER replaced by a different run's localPort change (each run owns its own entry), closing the cross-run kill. KEEP acquireRemoteMcpTunnel/releaseRemoteMcpTunnel (host-keyed, 21-81) verbatim for the local/non-pool/single-tenant fallback. STEP 3: add a max-concurrent-tunnels ceiling (configurable, default derived from port range) that throws a TYPED PortExhaustionError the manager maps to no_capacity; guard the recycled-port-vs-stale-entry interaction (38-47,96-100) under N concurrent live ports.

STEP 2 (NO behavior change to the existing function; ADD a per-run variant): keep acquireAgentMcpEndpoint exactly as-is (token+local-server-ref+host-keyed tunnel) for the local/single-tenant fallback. ADD acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun(settings, workerHost, runKey) that issues the token, refcounts the local server, and acquires the PER-RUN tunnel via workerHostPool.openForRun(...,runKey,...); its release() revokes the token, releases the local server, and calls workerHostPool.closeForRun(workerHost, runKey). This keeps ALL THREE sub-resources owned by ONE lease object (no orphaned token / local-server ref) - the central fix for critique gap #1.

STEP 1 NEW package @symphony/dispatch-coordinator (distinct from the pure-policy @symphony/dispatch). Deps: @symphony/worker-box-pool, @symphony/domain, @symphony/ports; type-only @symphony/mcp + @symphony/worker-host-pool for the injected McpEndpointManager port types so worker-box-pool never gains an mcp/tunnel runtime dep (invariant #8). The concrete McpEndpointManager is constructed in daemon.ts (which already may depend on mcp), NOT inside this package, keeping the dependency direction clean.

STEP 1: DispatchCoordinator class + createDispatchCoordinator. Wraps an injected BoxPool + injected McpEndpointManager. Owns acquireRunSlot, the synchronous capacityProbe() (governs->pool.isEnabled, canAcquire->pool.canAcquire), verbatim-delegating isEnabled/reconcile/hydrate/drain/snapshot, AND the authoritative reload-surviving per-(issueId,slotIndex,leaseId) RunSlot registry. capacityProbe() is built ONCE (stable identity) and re-reads live pool state each call so a reconcile never strands a stale probe captured by the orchestrator ctor. STEP 2: acquireRunSlot opens the endpoint after lease-bind; registers onMachineRecycling so a machine recycle fails open RunSlots on that box cleanly; drain() best-effort closes every surviving RunSlot's endpoint from the registry on a force-drain deadline (the unowned-orphan-tunnel fix). reconcile() rebuilds the registry view from pool.snapshot() so accounting cannot desync across reload. STEP 4: reconcile() calls pool.swapProvider(next) BEFORE pool.reconcile(next), and only when the provider construction actually changed.

STEP 1/2: RunSlot + createRunSlot wrapping a BoxLease (+ optional AgentMcpEndpointLease). Holds slotId/machineLeaseId/issueId/slotIndex/leaseId/workerHost/runKey/mcpEndpoint/acquiredAtMs; heartbeat() forwards to BoxLease.heartbeat(); release(outcome)/fail(reason) under an idempotent guard: (1) close THIS slot's endpoint via mcpEndpointManager.release(slot) exactly once, THEN (2) delegate to the wrapped BoxLease.release/fail (which is itself leaseId+settled+DESTROYED guarded), THEN (3) deregister from the coordinator registry. A machine-recycle-initiated fail short-circuits the endpoint close (the box is already gone) but still settles the lease and deregisters - exactly-once across BOTH the normal path and the recycle path.

STEP 1: the McpEndpointManager port { readonly perRunEndpoint: boolean; open(req): Promise<AgentMcpEndpointLease | null>; release(lease): Promise<void> } and a NULL passthrough (perRunEndpoint=false, open()->null, release()->noop) used by the step-1 passthrough. STEP 2: a concrete manager (perRunEndpoint=true) that for a remote (sshAddressable) host calls acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun(settings, workerHost, runKey) and on release calls lease.release(); for a local host returns null (the local path is untouched - acp keeps acquiring locally). perRunEndpoint is DERIVED from this concrete manager being wired, and is the capability the step-3 gate consumes.

STEP 1+: passthrough byte-identity (acquireRunSlot delegates to pool.acquire; the SAME typed no_capacity reasons; a thrown pool fault rethrows verbatim); RunSlot exactly-once release across normal+recycle paths; capacityProbe() shape equals the hand-built {governs,canAcquire} and identity is stable across reconcile; the runtime-event-string mapping contract (every no_capacity reason -> worker_host_capacity, throw -> box_pool_acquire_error).

STEP 2/3: two slots on one host get DISTINCT remotePorts; closeForRun(A) leaves B alive; a machine recycle for run A FAILS run A's slot cleanly (no hung endpoint, deregistered) while leaving run B's endpoint untouched; a stale late close (runKey embedded) is a no-op vs a newer run; a localPort change for one run never replaces another run's per-run entry; endpoint-open throw settles the just-acquired BoxLease healthy then rethrows as the structured acquire error with NO half-open ssh child; force-drain closes every registry endpoint; port-exhaustion ceiling -> typed no_capacity (not throw).

STEP 0: BoxPoolSettings gains slotsPerMachine (the canonical field at the 192 area) and exposes maxInFlight as a DERIVED getter returning slotsPerMachine (a getter, not a second writable field) so the two can never drift. Update the 191 comment from `the only value safe against the workerHost-keyed MCP tunnel refCount` to `safe for any value once each RunSlot owns its own MCP endpoint (token+local-server+tunnel); >1 is gated by a runtime capability + co-residence opt-in`. STEP 3: add optional `coResidence?: boolean` (operator opt-in for slotsPerMachine>1) and `maxConcurrentTunnels?: number` (the ceiling).

STEP 0: parseBoxPool (594) reads worker.box_pool.max_in_flight (key UNCHANGED) into slotsPerMachine; since maxInFlight is a getter over slotsPerMachine in the domain type, the constructed object is shape-stable (one own field, not two), so the absent-box_pool deep-equal-clone regression holds and config.test.ts only needs the slotsPerMachine assertion added. STEP 3: parse worker.box_pool.co_residence and worker.box_pool.max_concurrent_tunnels. validateDispatchConfig (459) keeps ONLY the static structural checks (it has no runtime capability) - the slotsPerMachine>1 capability gate moves to main.ts post-construction (see below).

STEP 1: add buildDispatchCoordinator(settings, env) beside buildBoxPool (55-67): it calls the existing createBoxPool(...) with the unchanged {clock,logEvent,ledgerPath} deps, then wraps it with the injected McpEndpointManager (step 1: null passthrough; step 2: the concrete acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun-backed manager). buildBoxPool STAYS (box-pool-wiring/e2e tests import it and the runtime can still accept a bare BoxPool).

STEP 1: swap `buildBoxPool` -> `buildDispatchCoordinator` at 118; pass coordinator to SymphonyRuntime; `coordinator?.hydrate()`; drainBoxPool finally delegates to coordinator.drain. STEP 3: AFTER the coordinator is constructed (so its perRunEndpoint capability exists), add a post-construction capability gate: if settings.worker.boxPool.slotsPerMachine>1 then REQUIRE coordinator.capabilities.perRunEndpoint===true AND settings.worker.boxPool.coResidence===true, else throw a clear startup error. This is the single mechanical safety; it lives where the capability is actually known (resolving critique gap #3).

STEP 1: SymphonyRuntimeOptions accepts EITHER input.coordinator OR input.boxPool; if only boxPool is given the ctor wraps it in a null-endpoint passthrough coordinator (low-churn: every existing boxPool-injecting test keeps working). The CapacityProbe (300-302) reads coordinator.capacityProbe(). runClaim's acquire block (501-541) becomes coordinator.acquireRunSlot(...); the discriminated result is mapped to the EXACT existing events: a THROW -> `dispatch_skipped ... box_pool_acquire_error` (519-525) verbatim; any `{status:'no_capacity'}` (regardless of reason) -> `dispatch_skipped ... worker_host_capacity` (531) verbatim. lease/heartbeatLease become slot/heartbeatSlot; the finally (635-647) becomes `await slot.release(boxOutcome)` (which closes the endpoint THEN settles the lease). setWorkerHost/abandonClaim/handle.release wiring preserved verbatim. STEP 2: thread slot.mcpEndpoint (the resolved AgentMcpEndpointLease, or null) into runAgentAttempt input so acp consumes it and SKIPS its own acquire+release.

STEP 2: RunAgentAttemptInput + executor.startSession gain an optional pre-resolved `mcpEndpoint` (the AgentMcpEndpointLease threaded from slot.mcpEndpoint). When present, acp.startSession uses it INSTEAD of acquireAgentMcpEndpoint AND acp.stopSession SKIPS `session.mcpEndpoint.release()` (a single ownership flag - the coordinator owns the whole lease, so acp must not double-release token/local-server/tunnel; this is the precise fix for critique gap #1). When absent (local host / non-pool fallback) acp behaves byte-for-byte as today (acquires AND releases). A session restart/resume within one run reuses the same threaded lease and never re-acquires.

STEP 1: re-export the coordinator surface (DispatchCoordinator, createDispatchCoordinator/buildDispatchCoordinator, RunSlot, McpEndpointManager) from @symphony/dispatch-coordinator alongside the existing worker-box-pool re-exports (176-190) so live/e2e and architecture-boundaries tests import them from @symphony/cli.
STEP 1: add { path: ./packages/dispatch-coordinator } to references (after ./packages/dispatch at line 20) and register the package in the pnpm workspace so tsc --build and pnpm install pick it up.

STEP 3: the gated co-residence slot suffix. Both workspacePath (line 22) and remoteWorkspacePath (370-373) today suffix slotIndex ONLY when ensembleSize>1, returning the BARE `<root>/<identifier>` otherwise (VERIFIED). Add an explicit `forceSlotSuffix` option (set by the coordinator only when slotsPerMachine>1 co-residence is active) so two co-resident same-issue slots NEVER share the bare workspace/resume path; the ensembleSize==1 single-slot layout (live-ssh/resume-state depend on it) is untouched when the flag is off.

STEP 1: the passthrough e2e stays green (mcpEndpoint=null, fake provider). STEP 3: a REAL N-per-machine multi-tenant e2e (slotsPerMachine=2, coResidence=true, fake provider + a fake McpEndpointManager handing out distinct ports) asserting two concurrent same-issue and cross-issue runs co-reside on one fake://box, both complete, per-issue RunSlot refcount is exact, overlapping box-second windows bill via liveLeaseAcquiredMs, and the gated slot suffix keeps the two workspaces distinct.

publicInterfaces.name: DispatchCoordinator (interface)
publicInterfaces.signature: interface DispatchCoordinator { acquireRunSlot(req: AcquireRunSlotRequest): Promise<{ status: 'bound'; slot: RunSlot } | { status: 'no_capacity'; reason: 'acquire_timeout' | 'spend_cap' | 'pool_disabled' | 'provider_error' | 'tunnel_exhausted' }>; capacityProbe(): CapacityProbe | undefined; isEnabled(): boolean; readonly capabilities: { perRunEndpoint: boolean }; reconcile(next: BoxPoolSettings): void; drain(opts: { deadlineMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal }): Promise<void>; hydrate(): Promise<void>; snapshot(): BoxPoolSnapshot & { slots: ReadonlyArray<{ slotId: string; machineLeaseId: string; issueId: string; slotIndex: number; workerHost: string; remotePort: number | null }> }; }

publicInterfaces.name: AcquireRunSlotRequest
publicInterfaces.signature: interface AcquireRunSlotRequest { issueId: string; slotIndex: number; labels: ReadonlyArray<string>; affinityKey?: string | null; timeoutMs: number; signal?: AbortSignal }

publicInterfaces.name: RunSlot
publicInterfaces.signature: interface RunSlot { readonly slotId: string; readonly machineLeaseId: string; readonly issueId: string; readonly slotIndex: number; readonly leaseId: string; readonly workerHost: string; readonly runKey: string; readonly mcpEndpoint: AgentMcpEndpointLease | null; readonly acquiredAtMs: number; heartbeat(): void; release(outcome: BoxOutcome): Promise<void>; fail(reason: string): Promise<void>; }

publicInterfaces.name: McpEndpointManager (injected port)
publicInterfaces.signature: interface McpEndpointManager { readonly perRunEndpoint: boolean; open(req: { settings: Settings; workerHost: string; runKey: string }): Promise<AgentMcpEndpointLease | null>; release(lease: AgentMcpEndpointLease | null): Promise<void>; }

publicInterfaces.name: createDispatchCoordinator
publicInterfaces.signature: function createDispatchCoordinator(deps: { pool: BoxPool; mcpEndpointManager: McpEndpointManager; settings: BoxPoolSettings }): DispatchCoordinator

publicInterfaces.name: buildDispatchCoordinator (daemon.ts)
publicInterfaces.signature: function buildDispatchCoordinator(settings: Settings, env?: NodeJS.ProcessEnv): DispatchCoordinator | undefined

publicInterfaces.name: acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun (mcp, NEW; existing acquireAgentMcpEndpoint untouched)
publicInterfaces.signature: function acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun(settings: Settings, workerHost: string, runKey: string): Promise<AgentMcpEndpointLease>

publicInterfaces.name: WorkerHostPool.openForRun / closeForRun (host-keyed methods kept verbatim)
publicInterfaces.signature: openForRun(workerHost: string, runKey: string, localHost: string, localPort: number): RemoteMcpTunnelLease; closeForRun(workerHost: string, runKey: string): void

publicInterfaces.name: BoxPool.onMachineRecycling (optional callback registration for the recycle-vs-endpoint ordering invariant)
BoxRecord.originProvider (added; provider that provisioned this box, for swap-safe destroy)
BoxPoolSettings.slotsPerMachine + maxInFlight getter (domain)
publicInterfaces.signature: interface BoxPoolSettings { /* ...unchanged... */ slotsPerMachine: number; /** @deprecated derived getter over slotsPerMachine */ readonly maxInFlight: number; coResidence?: boolean; maxConcurrentTunnels?: number; }

publicInterfaces.name: CapacityProbe (unchanged shape, now produced by coordinator, stable identity)
publicInterfaces.signature: interface CapacityProbe { governs(): boolean; canAcquire(): boolean }

tasks.title: Rename maxInFlight -> slotsPerMachine in domain; maxInFlight as a derived getter (drift-proof)
tasks.detail: In domain/src/index.ts BoxPoolSettings (180-224) make slotsPerMachine the canonical field and expose maxInFlight as a getter returning slotsPerMachine (NOT a second writable field), so a fixture that sets only one cannot desync the select logic and the gate. Update the 191 comment to reflect per-run-endpoint safety gated by capability + co-residence opt-in. No behavior change.

tasks.title: Parse max_in_flight into slotsPerMachine; keep config object shape-stable
tasks.detail: config/src/index.ts parseBoxPool (594): set slotsPerMachine from worker.box_pool.max_in_flight (positiveInt default 1; key UNCHANGED). Because maxInFlight is a getter over slotsPerMachine, the constructed settings object has ONE own field (slotsPerMachine), so the absent-box_pool deep-equal-clone regression holds. Update config.test.ts (485/512) to assert slotsPerMachine===3 (and that maxInFlight getter mirrors it). Update the absent-case test only if it shape-asserts.

tasks.title: Alias BoxRecord->MachineLease; read slotsPerMachine in the pool select path
tasks.detail: worker-box-pool/src/types.ts: add `export type MachineLease = BoxRecord;`; export from the barrel. pool.ts: isLeasable/pickRecord/canAcquire (56,257,574) read settings.slotsPerMachine (now mirrored by the getter, so no value changes). Keep BoxRecord canonical and BoxLease verbatim. Run the worker-box-pool suite unchanged to prove byte-identity.

tasks.title: Scaffold @symphony/dispatch-coordinator (distinct from @symphony/dispatch)
tasks.detail: Create package.json/tsconfig/src; add to ts/tsconfig.json references (after ./packages/dispatch) + pnpm workspace. Runtime deps: worker-box-pool, domain, ports. Type-only deps: mcp (AgentMcpEndpointLease) + worker-host-pool (RemoteMcpTunnelLease). Do NOT touch the existing pure-policy @symphony/dispatch.

tasks.title: RunSlot + McpEndpointManager port + null passthrough manager + registry
tasks.detail: runSlot.ts: wrap a BoxLease (mcpEndpoint=null in step 1) with an idempotent release/fail/heartbeat that on settle closes the endpoint (noop for null) THEN delegates to BoxLease THEN deregisters. mcpEndpointManager.ts: the port + a null passthrough (perRunEndpoint=false, open->null, release->noop). The coordinator holds the reload-surviving per-(issueId,slotIndex,leaseId) RunSlot registry. Unit-test exactly-once release (normal + double-release no-op) and heartbeat forwarding.

tasks.title: DispatchCoordinator 1:1 passthrough reproducing acquire->settle AND the two event strings
tasks.detail: acquireRunSlot calls pool.acquire; on leased wraps the BoxLease in a RunSlot (mcpEndpoint=null) registered in the registry; maps a THROW to a rethrow and every no_capacity reason to a single discriminated no_capacity result. Explicitly document+test that the RUNTIME maps {throw->box_pool_acquire_error, any no_capacity->worker_host_capacity} so the green runtime.test event-string assertions hold (closing critique gap #2). capacityProbe() built once, stable identity, re-reads live pool. isEnabled/reconcile/hydrate/drain delegate verbatim; snapshot appends an empty slots list. capabilities.perRunEndpoint = manager.perRunEndpoint (false here).

tasks.title: Wire buildDispatchCoordinator; runtime accepts coordinator OR boxPool (low-churn)
tasks.detail: daemon.ts: buildDispatchCoordinator (createBoxPool internally + null McpEndpointManager in step 1). main.ts: coordinator replaces boxPool at 118-127,169. runtime/src/index.ts: accept input.coordinator OR input.boxPool (wrap a bare boxPool in a null-endpoint passthrough coordinator in the ctor so every existing boxPool-injecting test stays green); CapacityProbe (300-302) from coordinator.capacityProbe(); runClaim 501-541 -> coordinator.acquireRunSlot mapping throw->box_pool_acquire_error and no_capacity->worker_host_capacity verbatim; finally 635-647 -> slot.release(boxOutcome); lease/heartbeatLease -> slot/heartbeatSlot. cli/src/index.ts: re-export coordinator surface. Absent-coordinator path == absent-boxPool today.

tasks.title: Keep runtime/orchestrator/box-pool-wiring/e2e-memory green under the passthrough
tasks.detail: Run the full suite. Because the runtime accepts the bare boxPool field (wrapping internally), test injection sites need no churn. Confirm runtime.test event-string assertions (box_pool_acquire_error, worker_host_capacity) still pass. orchestrator.ts unchanged (same CapacityProbe shape + stable identity).

tasks.title: Per-run tunnel methods on WorkerHostPool (re-key by workerHost#runKey); keep host-keyed
tasks.detail: Add openForRun/closeForRun re-keyed by `${workerHost}#${runKey}`, distinct remotePort per run, no host-coalescing. Each per-run entry is matched on its full key so a different run's localPort change never replaces it (closing the localPort-swap cross-run kill, dossier mode 3). KEEP acquireRemoteMcpTunnel/releaseRemoteMcpTunnel verbatim for fallback. Unit-test distinct ports, independent close, and a localPort change for run A leaving run B's entry intact.

tasks.title: acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun in mcp (whole-lease per-run; existing fn untouched)
tasks.detail: Add acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun(settings, workerHost, runKey): issues the token, refcounts the local server, acquires the per-run tunnel via openForRun; its release() revokes the token, releases the local server, calls closeForRun. This keeps token+local-server-ref+tunnel owned by ONE lease (critique gap #1). Leave acquireAgentMcpEndpoint exactly as-is for the local/fallback path. Unit-test that release() does all three (token revoked, local server ref dropped, closeForRun called).

tasks.title: Concrete McpEndpointManager; open-after-bind / close-before-settle; recycle ordering invariant
tasks.detail: mcpEndpointManager.ts concrete (perRunEndpoint=true): open()-> for sshAddressable host calls acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun, for local host returns null. coordinator.acquireRunSlot: after lease-bind, open the endpoint (runKey from the verified-unique (issueId,slotIndex)); on open throw, settle the just-acquired BoxLease HEALTHY then rethrow as the structured acquire error (no machine-lease leak, no half-open child). RunSlot.release: close endpoint THEN settle lease. Register coordinator.onMachineRecycling via pool.onMachineRecycling so a poison/recycle of a box fails its open RunSlots cleanly (endpoint closed, lease settled, deregistered) and returns a clean failure to sibling runs (closing critique gaps #5 and the recycle-vs-tunnel hazard). daemon.ts injects the concrete manager.

tasks.title: Route acp through slot.mcpEndpoint; acp SKIPS its own acquire AND release when threaded
tasks.detail: Thread slot.mcpEndpoint (the AgentMcpEndpointLease) through RunAgentAttemptInput into executor.startSession. When present acp.startSession uses it instead of acquireAgentMcpEndpoint AND acp.stopSession SKIPS session.mcpEndpoint.release() (single ownership flag - coordinator owns the whole lease, preventing the double-release/orphan of critique gap #1). When absent (local host) acp behaves byte-for-byte as today. Session restart/resume reuses the threaded lease. snapshot() surfaces live RunSlot remotePorts.

tasks.title: Tunnel/endpoint isolation tests (distinct ports, independent close, recycle clean-fail, stale, drain)
tasks.detail: coordinator + worker-host-pool tests: two slots on one host get distinct ports; closeForRun(A) leaves B alive; a machine recycle of run A fails run A cleanly (no hung endpoint to a dead host) and leaves B untouched; a stale late close (runKey embedded) no-ops vs a newer run; endpoint-open throw settles healthy + rethrows with no half-open child; a stall during the open window aborts open, settles the lease, closes any half-open endpoint; force-drain closes every registry endpoint; acp consumes the threaded endpoint and never double-releases. Single-slot still allocates exactly one endpoint per machine so live-ssh/e2e are unchanged.

tasks.title: Two-part GATE: static config checks + post-construction capability+opt-in gate in main
tasks.detail: validateDispatchConfig keeps ONLY static checks (no runtime capability). Add the slotsPerMachine>1 gate in main.ts AFTER buildDispatchCoordinator: require coordinator.capabilities.perRunEndpoint===true AND settings.worker.boxPool.coResidence===true, else throw a clear startup error. This resolves critique gap #3 (capability is known only post-construction) and risk #1 (co-residence is an explicit operator opt-in, not just a capability). Parse co_residence + max_concurrent_tunnels in config. Add the max-concurrent-tunnels ceiling in worker-host-pool that surfaces as a TYPED no_capacity (reason 'tunnel_exhausted'), not a throw. Tests: >1 with no capability throws; >1 with capability but no opt-in throws; >1 with both passes; default 1 always passes; ceiling -> no_capacity.

tasks.title: Gated co-residence slot suffix (local+remote); verified (issueId,slotIndex) uniqueness -> runKey
tasks.detail: Add forceSlotSuffix to workspacePath (line 22) AND remoteWorkspacePath (370-373) so two co-resident same-issue slots never share the bare `<root>/<identifier>` (VERIFIED both suffix only when ensembleSize>1). The coordinator sets it only when slotsPerMachine>1 co-residence is active, NEVER altering the ensembleSize==1 layout. Add an explicit test that the coordinator never places two slots with the SAME (issueId, slotIndex) on one host (the runKey uniqueness invariant), constructing the actual maxBoxesPerIssue/slotsPerMachine same-issue collision the dossier warns about (closing critique gap #4). runKey = `${slotIndex}` scoped per machine; assert workspace-suffix and tunnel-key uniqueness hold SIMULTANEOUSLY.

tasks.title: N-per-machine multi-tenant + billing + per-issue accounting + port-ceiling tests
tasks.detail: Real N-per-machine e2e (slotsPerMachine=2, coResidence=true, fake provider + fake distinct-port manager): two concurrent same-issue AND cross-issue runs co-reside on one fake://box, both complete; box-seconds bill overlapping windows (liveLeaseAcquiredMs); per-issue RunSlot refcount is exact (maxBoxesPerIssue stays the MACHINE-level cap, the registry adds precise per-issue slot accounting); tunnel ports distinct; a poisoned box fails all its co-resident slots cleanly. Add a port-exhaustion-ceiling test asserting typed no_capacity. Document the shared-machine-poison blast-radius tradeoff at the gate (risk #6).

tasks.title: BoxPool.swapProvider with per-box origin capture (no orphaned paid box)
tasks.detail: Add originProvider to BoxRecord and swapProvider(next): BEFORE reassigning, stamp originProvider on every existing record (the provider that provisioned it); mark old-provider boxes for drain; reassign this.provider=resolveProvider(...) (drop readonly at 137); re-thread reaperInternals.provider; rebuild the ledger usesLedger gate WITHOUT reconstructing the singleton. Modify recycle() (973) and destroyDescriptor to destroy against `record.originProvider ?? this.provider` so an in-flight lease settling AFTER the swap routes destroy to its ORIGINAL backend (closing critique gap #7 / invariant #5: never orphan a paid box). coordinator.reconcile runs swapProvider before pool.reconcile, only when provider construction changed.

tasks.title: Provider-swap tests (rebuild-on-change, skip-on-same, in-flight settles to ORIGINAL provider)
tasks.detail: reconcile changing provider rebuilds it and routes new provisions to the new provider while old-provider boxes drain UNDER THE OLD PROVIDER; same-provider reconcile skips the swap (no rebuild); an in-flight lease during a provider change settles cleanly and its box is destroyed against its captured originProvider (not the new this.provider) and is never orphaned; reaperInternals.provider and ledger usesLedger re-threaded after swap.

cases: worker-box-pool pool/pool-props/lease/ledger/mutex/reaper/registry + providers suites pass unchanged after BoxRecord->MachineLease alias and slotsPerMachine read
config.test.ts: absent box_pool still deep-equal clone (slotsPerMachine is the only own field; maxInFlight is a getter, so object shape is stable)
config.test.ts: max_in_flight:3 yields slotsPerMachine===3 and the maxInFlight getter mirrors it
domain.test.ts: maxInFlight getter always equals slotsPerMachine (cannot drift)
acquireRunSlot delegates to pool.acquire and returns the SAME typed no_capacity reasons
RunSlot.release with mcpEndpoint=null delegates to BoxLease.release/fail with identical poison/healthy classification
capacityProbe() shape == hand-built {governs,canAcquire} AND identity is stable across reconcile (orchestrator captured-probe is never stale)
thrown pool fault rethrows verbatim -> runtime emits box_pool_acquire_error (abandonClaim+handle.release, no run/history/lease)
EVERY no_capacity reason -> runtime emits the single worker_host_capacity dispatch_skipped (no per-reason event differentiation, matching today)
absent coordinator path == absent boxPool today; bare boxPool injection is wrapped in a null-endpoint passthrough coordinator (no test-injection churn)
runtime.test + orchestrator.test + box-pool-wiring + e2e-memory stay green
RunSlot release is exactly-once across BOTH normal and machine-recycle paths

testMatrix.area: Step 2 endpoint isolation (multi-tenant, all THREE sub-resources)
cases: acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun.release() revokes the token, drops the local-server refcount, AND closes the per-run tunnel (no orphaned sub-resource)
acp consumes the threaded slot.mcpEndpoint and SKIPS its own acquire AND its own release (no double-close/refcount underflow)
two slots on one host get DISTINCT remotePorts via openForRun
closeForRun(A) leaves B's tunnel alive (no host-coalescing last-ref-kill)
a localPort change for run A never replaces run B's per-run entry (dossier mode-3 fixed for per-run keys)

a machine recycle/poison for run A fails run A's slot CLEANLY (endpoint closed, lease settled, deregistered, clean failure surfaced) and leaves run B untouched (recycle-vs-endpoint ordering invariant)
endpoint-open throw settles the just-acquired BoxLease healthy and rethrows the structured acquire error with NO half-open ssh child
a stall during the open-endpoint window aborts open, settles the lease, and closes any half-opened endpoint
force-drain closes every surviving registry endpoint on the deadline (no leaked ssh -N children on shutdown)
local-host path (workerHost null) unaffected: acp acquires AND releases its own endpoint byte-for-byte
host-keyed acquireRemoteMcpTunnel/releaseRemoteMcpTunnel still work for local/non-pool fallback
single-slot allocates exactly one endpoint per machine (live-ssh/e2e unchanged)
validateDispatchConfig keeps only static checks; slotsPerMachine>1 is NOT gated there (no runtime capability available pre-construction)
main.ts post-construction gate: slotsPerMachine>1 with perRunEndpoint=false throws
slotsPerMachine>1 with perRunEndpoint=true but coResidence=false throws (blast-radius is an explicit opt-in)
slotsPerMachine>1 with both perRunEndpoint and coResidence passes
default slotsPerMachine=1 always passes (gate never triggers)
REAL N-per-machine e2e: slotsPerMachine=2, two same-issue AND two cross-issue runs co-reside on one fake://box, all complete
gated forceSlotSuffix keeps two co-resident same-issue workspaces distinct (local AND remote path) and NEVER alters the ensembleSize==1 bare layout

coordinator never places two slots with the same (issueId, slotIndex) on one host; runKey + workspace-suffix uniqueness hold simultaneously (the dossier same-issue collision is constructed and asserted, not merely claimed)
per-issue RunSlot accounting is an exact refcount of live slots; maxBoxesPerIssue stays the MACHINE-level cap
port/tunnel-exhaustion ceiling -> typed no_capacity ('tunnel_exhausted'), never an unhandled throw inside acquireRunSlot; recycled-port-vs-stale-entry guarded
overlapping box-second windows bill correctly for N concurrent leases (liveLeaseAcquiredMs)
a poisoned co-resident box fails ALL its slots cleanly (shared-machine-poison correlation surfaced as clean per-run failures, documented tradeoff)
reconcile changing provider rebuilds it in place (resolveProvider re-run) without reconstructing the singleton
new provisions route to the new provider; old-provider boxes drain UNDER THE OLD PROVIDER
same-provider reconcile skips the swap (no rebuild)
an in-flight lease settling AFTER the swap destroys its box against record.originProvider (its ORIGINAL backend), never the new this.provider; box not orphaned
reaperInternals.provider and ledger usesLedger re-threaded after swap
pending:// sentinel kept; cleanupWorkerHost maps it to null; snapshot surfaces the literal sentinel; finish->RetryEntry affinity tolerant
orchestrator stays pure/synchronous (claim/setWorkerHost/abandonClaim/finish do zero I/O); CapacityProbe is the only sync seam and has stable identity
lease settled exactly once in runClaim finally alongside handle.release + finish; poison/healthy classification unchanged; endpoint closed before lease settle
generation/activity guards + per-box mutex hold; stale late settle / stale late endpoint-close is a no-op
hardened internals (reservations/mutex/reaper/drain-epoch/ledger/spend/liveLeaseAcquiredMs) byte-for-byte
drain never leaks a paid cloud box AND never leaks an ssh -N child; coordinator.drain closes orphan endpoints from the registry
disabled-but-present pool/coordinator falls through to static/local (governs()===false); absent path byte-identical
coordinator + RunSlot registry survive config reload (built once in main; reconcile rebuilds the registry view from pool.snapshot(), no desync)

migrationSteps: STEP 0 (pure rename, lowest-risk, NO new package): T0a/T0b/T0c. Alias BoxRecord->MachineLease (BoxRecord stays canonical/re-exported); rename maxInFlight->slotsPerMachine in domain with maxInFlight as a DERIVED GETTER (drift-proof, single own field so the config object shape is stable); make the pool select path read slotsPerMachine. Suite byte-identical: absent box_pool => default 1 => same deep-equal clone; green worker-box-pool/provider suites compile unchanged. GATE: pnpm typecheck && pnpm exec vitest run && pnpm lint.

STEP 1 (coordinator as 1:1 PASSTHROUGH, mcpEndpoint=null, default slotsPerMachine=1): T1a-T1e. Create @symphony/dispatch-coordinator (distinct from the pure-policy @symphony/dispatch), wrap BoxPool + a null McpEndpointManager, mint a RunSlot with mcpEndpoint=null, reproduce acquire->setWorkerHost->settle AND the EXACT two runtime event strings (throw->box_pool_acquire_error, every no_capacity->worker_host_capacity) so this is genuinely byte-identical at the runtime boundary (NOT just at the pool boundary). The runtime keeps accepting a bare boxPool (wrapped internally) so no test-injection site churns. Coordinator + registry are a reload-surviving singleton built once in daemon/main. capacityProbe identity is stable across reconcile. GATE: pnpm typecheck && pnpm exec vitest run && pnpm lint.

STEP 2 (per-run WHOLE-endpoint isolation - token+local-server+tunnel - distinct ports): T2a-T2e. Add openForRun/closeForRun to WorkerHostPool re-keyed by workerHost#runKey (no host-coalescing; per-run-keyed entries immune to another run's localPort change; host-keyed methods kept for fallback); add acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun in mcp owning all THREE sub-resources behind one lease; coordinator opens the whole endpoint AFTER lease-bind and closes it BEFORE lease-settle via the injected manager; acp consumes the threaded lease and SKIPS BOTH its own acquire AND its own release (single ownership flag - no double-close, no orphaned token/local-server ref). A pool onMachineRecycling callback fails a recycled box's open RunSlots cleanly so a poisoned machine returns a clean per-run failure instead of a hung endpoint to a dead host. Single-slot still allocates exactly one endpoint per machine so live-ssh/e2e are unchanged. GATE: pnpm typecheck && pnpm exec vitest run && pnpm lint.

STEP 3 (unlock slotsPerMachine>1 behind a TWO-PART gate): T3a-T3c. Static checks stay in validateDispatchConfig; the capability+opt-in gate moves to main.ts AFTER the coordinator is constructed (where perRunEndpoint is actually known) and requires BOTH perRunEndpoint AND an explicit coResidence opt-in (blast-radius is an operator tradeoff). Force the gated unconditional slot suffix on BOTH local and remote workspace paths for same-issue co-residence, with the (issueId,slotIndex) uniqueness invariant feeding runKey and proven by a constructed-collision test. Add the typed tunnel-exhaustion ceiling (no_capacity, not a throw) and the real N-per-machine multi-tenant + per-issue accounting + overlapping-billing tests. Default stays 1. GATE: pnpm typecheck && pnpm exec vitest run && pnpm lint.

STEP 4 (Finding #1, independent of steps 1-3, lands any time after step 0): T4a-T4b. swapProvider captures originProvider PER BOX before reassigning this.provider, and recycle/destroyDescriptor route to record.originProvider ?? this.provider so an in-flight lease settling after the swap destroys against its ORIGINAL backend and never orphans a paid box. coordinator.reconcile runs swapProvider before pool.reconcile only when provider construction changed. Tests cover rebuild-on-change, skip-on-same, and the in-flight-settles-to-original-provider case. GATE: pnpm typecheck && pnpm exec vitest run && pnpm lint.

BACKWARD-COMPAT throughout: slotsPerMachine default 1 + mcpEndpoint=null make the coordinator a 1:1 passthrough byte-identical to today at every step; each step is independently shippable AND reversible; the runtime keeps accepting a bare boxPool for one release to avoid mass test churn.
subsumes:
FINDING #1 (provider not rebuilt on reload - VERIFIED: pool.ts:137 `private readonly provider`, resolved once at 161 in the ctor; reconcile (289-324) swaps settings but never re-runs resolveProvider) is SUBSUMED by T4a/T4b via coordinator.reconcile -> pool.swapProvider then pool.reconcile. swapProvider is NARROW: it captures `originProvider` on every record BEFORE reassigning, marks old-provider boxes for drain, reassigns this.provider=resolveProvider(next.provider,next,deps) (drops readonly at 137), re-threads reaperInternals.provider, and rebuilds the ledger usesLedger gate WITHOUT reconstructing the singleton. CRUCIALLY, recycle() (973) and destroyDescriptor are changed to destroy against `record.originProvider ?? this.provider`, because recycle reads this.provider AT SETTLE TIME (verified) - an in-flight lease that settles after the swap would otherwise route destroy() to the NEW provider and orphan a paid box (the latent bug the prior plan missed; critique gap #7 / invariant #5). The swap fires only when provider construction actually changed; same-provider reconcile skips it.

FINDING #3 (per-issue accounting over slots, not machines - VERIFIED: leaseIssues:Set<string> on the record + reservedProvisionsByIssue/issueWouldExceedCap at pool.ts:635-646 count MACHINES an issue touches, not run slots) is SUBSUMED by making RunSlot first-class: the coordinator holds the authoritative per-(issueId,slotIndex,leaseId) RunSlot registry (a reload-surviving singleton, rebuilt from pool.snapshot() on reconcile so it cannot desync), so per-issue fairness becomes a precise refcount of live RunSlots; maxBoxesPerIssue stays the MACHINE-level cap with NO change to the hardened machine-level reservation single-flight.

The two findings join at the slot layer. The ensembleSize==1 bare-workspace edge (VERIFIED: workspacePath line 22 AND remoteWorkspacePath 370-373 both suffix slotIndex ONLY when ensembleSize>1, returning the bare `<root>/<identifier>` otherwise) is closed by the SAME step-3 forceSlotSuffix sharding that fires only when two slots of one issue may co-reside, strictly gated so it never alters the single-slot layout, with a constructed (issueId,slotIndex)-collision test proving runKey and workspace-suffix uniqueness hold simultaneously.

The central correction over the prior revision: the per-run resource is the WHOLE AgentMcpEndpointLease (token+refcounted-local-server+tunnel, VERIFIED bundled in agentEndpoint.ts:34-68 and released together in acp.stopSession:280 inside the runner finally), not 'the tunnel'. The coordinator owns the whole lease via acquireAgentMcpEndpointForRun and acp is patched to skip BOTH its acquire and its release when threaded - eliminating the double-close / orphaned-token / orphaned-local-server-ref hazard the prior plan's tunnel-only split would have introduced (critique gap #1).
openQuestions: runKey is chosen as `${slotIndex}` scoped per machine (stable across a run, matches the workspace slot suffix). Confirm the coordinator's (issueId,slotIndex) uniqueness invariant holds on EVERY claim path (the constructed-collision test in T3b is the proof). leaseId is unique per stamp but rotates on retry, so it is NOT used for the workspace-shared runKey; if a future path could place two slots with the same (issueId,slotIndex) on one host, runKey must fall back to leaseId AND the workspace suffix must follow - decide whether to assert-and-reject the collision (preferred) vs. silently disambiguate.

The post-construction capability gate lives in main.ts (after buildDispatchCoordinator) because validateDispatchConfig(settings) has no runtime capability (VERIFIED: called at main.ts:112 BEFORE buildBoxPool at 118). Confirm there is no OTHER call site of validateDispatchConfig that must also enforce slotsPerMachine>1 (the runtime calls it per-poll at index.ts:398) - the per-poll call should NOT re-check the capability (the coordinator already exists and was gated once at startup); confirm the per-poll validateDispatchConfig stays capability-free so a reload raising slotsPerMachine>1 without restarting is caught at the next startup, not silently in-poll. If in-poll enforcement is desired, the coordinator must expose its capability to the runtime's reload path.

The runtime keeps accepting a bare boxPool field (wrapped in a null-endpoint passthrough coordinator) for one release to minimize test churn. Confirm we WANT the eventual hard rename input.boxPool->input.coordinator as a follow-up cleanup, and that no consumer relies on reading input.boxPool back out (it becomes an internal wrap).

ssh -N child + remote-port fan-out grows from 1/host to N/host (starting 46000). The typed tunnel-exhaustion ceiling (no_capacity 'tunnel_exhausted') and the recycled-port-vs-stale-entry guard land in step 2/3 BEFORE the default can be raised above 1. Confirm the ceiling default is derived from a sane fd/process budget and load-test fd/process pressure on a busy controller (many hosts x N slots) before any operator raises slotsPerMachine>1.

slotsPerMachine>1 multiplies the blast radius of a poisoned machine across N concurrent runs (one bad box fails N co-resident runs on recycle). Per-run endpoint isolation prevents cross-run endpoint KILLS and the onMachineRecycling callback turns a recycle into clean per-run failures, but it does NOT remove the shared-machine-poison correlation itself. This is why the gate requires an explicit coResidence opt-in (not just capability) and the tradeoff is documented at the gate. Confirm operators accept that correlated failure mode before defaulting >1.

The concrete McpEndpointManager is constructed in daemon.ts (which may depend on @symphony/mcp) and injected, keeping worker-box-pool free of any mcp/tunnel runtime dep (invariant #8). Confirm daemon.ts is the right ownership boundary and that the type-only imports in @symphony/dispatch-coordinator (AgentMcpEndpointLease from mcp, RemoteMcpTunnelLease from worker-host-pool) stay erased so no runtime cycle forms.
